documentation.title: Internal regular mesh description (2D)
documentation.description:
Describes a regular mesh in XML, cfs generates the mesh during runtime.
Limited to regular meshes of rectangular or hexahedral first-order elements (use p-FEM if higher order is needed). For 2D and 3D.
Configure by * box (optional), default is max=1 and min = 0 * elements (or spacing): Give the number of elements in each direction. Only nx is mandatory, square/cubic elements are the default. * spacing (or elements): Give the element size in m. Only hx is mandatory; by default hy/hz = hx.
The sides have the following named nodes (left, right, bottom, top, front, back). The edges are labeled as bottom_left, bottom_right, ...
In 'regular' the name of the region is specified. One can define arbitrarily many 'subRegion' using any method by which one can define an 'elemList' in 'domain'.
The same 'elemList' and 'nodeList' as in 'domain' can additionally be specified, on top of any defined in 'domain'. This is especially useful when 'regular' is given by an external file via 'fileName' or by the command line (no 'input' needed then) See the test case 'ExternalRegularMesh3D'.
Almost everywhere math parser expressions can be used plus the constants defined optionally in the later 'domain/variableList'.
